description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a polygonal stone base and a pitched roof made of wooden beams and colorful, tiled shingles in alternating red and blue hues. The well's stone base is textured to resemble rough, natural rock, and the interior contains a visible pool of water with a rippling surface. Two vertical wooden posts support the roof, which is constructed from brown wooden planks and features a triangular gable design. A simple metal crank handle extends from one side, suggesting a manual water-drawing mechanism, though no bucket or rope is visible. The overall structure is robust and utilitarian, with no visible decorative embellishments, and is designed to provide access to groundwater in an urban or park setting.
description: A stone water well with a colorful tiled roof, wooden supports, and a metal crank handle, featuring a visible pool of water inside. The design is sturdy and functional, with no decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone base, water surface, two wooden supports, colorful tiled roof, and a metal crank handle on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular wooden roof structure, the open well interior, and the absence of the crank handle on this side.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the crank handle now on the right, showing the same stone base, water, and roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the wooden roof structure and the crank handle mechanism attached to the central post.
attribute_well_cover_design: A pitched roof made of wooden beams and colorful ceramic tiles; no lid or grating covers the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A metal crank handle is present, attached to the side, but no bucket or rope is visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a tall, polygonal stone wall serving as a protective barrier.
